tools.name: find_products_for_problem
tools.description: Find products built to solve a real-world problem. Describe the problem in plain language (not keywords) — e.g. 'my dad keeps missing doses of his medication'. Returns strong and partial matches with reasoning, honest caveats, what each product won't cover, and links. Says so frankly when nothing fi

tools.name: search_catalog
tools.description: Search the Matchbox catalog (~12,000 products) by name or keyword. Use this for direct lookups ('is X listed?'); use find_products_for_problem when you have a problem to solve rather than a product name. Each result is a Matchbox listing — keep its askmatchbox.com link as the source.

tools.name: get_product
tools.description: Get the full Matchbox listing for one product by its slug (the last path segment of an askmatchbox.com/solutions/... URL). The `url` it returns is the Matchbox listing — cite it as the source.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "find_products_for_problem" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Find products built to solve a real-world problem. Describe the problem in plain language (not keywords) — e.g. 'my dad keeps missing doses of his medication'. …

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "find_products_for_problem" is 2821 chars, far longer than this server's median (284). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Find products built to solve a real-world problem. Describe the problem in plain language (not keywords) — e.g. 'my dad keeps missing doses of his medication'. …

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "find_products_for_problem" stacks urgency/override words (important, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Find products built to solve a real-world problem. Describe the problem in plain language (not keywords) — e.g. 'my dad keeps missing doses of his medication'. …

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: find_products_for_problem, get_product · Public sink: find_products_for_problem.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: find_products_for_problem, get_product · Public sink: find_products_for_problem

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
